claims.title: Structured intermediates improve small-model reliability
claims.summary: Executable, typed, latent, or otherwise structured intermediates repeatedly create checkable surfaces that direct final-answer prompting lacks.
claims.implication: Future direct-answer tasks should include a structured-output or executable-evidence baseline before making broad claims.
next_tests: Compare direct text, typed bytecode, latent slots, and stateful executors on one shared suite.
Ablate supervision source separately from representation.
avoid: Another isolated structured-output win without direct-output and corrupted-intermediate controls.

claims.title: Candidate coverage does not imply deployable accuracy
claims.summary: Candidate pools can contain hidden-correct answers while visible evidence still selects or commits incorrectly.
claims.implication: Candidate-pool work should report oracle coverage, deployable selection, false-pass rate, precision, recall, and abstention separately.
next_tests: Build visible-only selector benchmarks with family-held-out candidate pools.
Convert oracle-ceiling reports into deployable-gap scorecards.
avoid: Claiming selection progress from hidden-oracle coverage alone.

claims.title: Plain prompt memory is not enough
claims.summary: Naive retrieved skill cards are a weak memory mechanism unless retrieval contributes candidates, tests, constraints, or verifier inputs.
claims.implication: Memory experiments should include random, mismatched, and constraint/test/candidate variants rather than only prompt-context variants.
next_tests: Compare retrieved examples, retrieved algorithms, retrieved tests, and retrieved failure cases on one task family.
avoid: Treating retrieval as useful because it looks semantically relevant but is never verified.

claims.title: Active evidence must be coupled to downstream decisions
claims.summary: Actively selected examples and probes can help, but the acquisition objective must optimize the final selector, verifier, or controller decision.
claims.implication: Evidence-acquisition work should report downstream decision lift per evidence budget, not only probe informativeness.
next_tests: Compare active probe policies by selector lift under identical budgets.
Separate expected-output-free probes from oracle-only diagnostics.
avoid: Optimizing examples or probes without showing commit, repair, or selection improvement.

claims.title: Adaptation must beat strong frozen alternatives
claims.summary: LoRA, DPO, distillation, GRPO, and DAgger are promising, but their value is unclear unless compared against frozen sampling, verification, retrieval, and tool-control alternatives.
claims.implication: Post-training runs should include frozen inference-time alternatives and explicit deployability boundaries.
next_tests: Run one update method against frozen inference-time alternatives on the same selector or compiler benchmark.
avoid: Adapter wins whose labels are hidden-only or whose artifacts are not auditable.

claims.title: Controls are the difference between a result and a story
claims.summary: The strongest imported lessons come from experiments that separate mechanism from confounders through corrupted, shuffled, random, frozen, length-held-out, or family-held-out controls.
claims.implication: Future claims should name the mechanism-falsifying control before running expensive work.
next_tests: Build a shared generalization and control suite used across compiler, selector, memory, and adaptation work.
avoid: Treating train or IID lift as evidence for generalization.

claims.title: Reliability requires explicit hidden-label and artifact boundaries
claims.summary: The repository should preserve small reproducible artifacts while excluding trained adapters and labeling oracle-only evidence separately from deployable evidence.
claims.implication: Reports and scaffolds must separate deployable evidence from hidden-oracle evaluation, and trained adapter directories must remain out of git.
next_tests: Add stronger hidden-label boundary and artifact-manifest checks as new experiments are created.
avoid: Checking in trained adapters or letting oracle-only metrics become headline deployment claims.

claims.title: The repository itself is a research instrument
claims.summary: Program charters, generated indexes, claim ledgers, scorecards, intake notes, templates, validation, and CI are part of the experiment system because they determine whether future work compounds.
claims.implication: New experiments should update program evidence and shared claims when they change what future work should believe.
next_tests: Measure whether scorecards and intake notes reduce duplicate proposals and improve citation of prior evidence.
avoid: Treating process as complete when it no longer improves research memory.

claims.title: Native thinking is an unused deployable lever; at the efficient budget its accuracy gain is coherent reasoning
claims.summary: Enabling Qwen3.5-4B's native thinking (disabled corpus-wide) raises deployable MBPP greedy pass@1 +15pp (0.76->0.91); accuracy is non-monotonic (optimum ~512-1024, then decline; unbudgeted worse than a cap). A visible-test budget controller is an efficiency (not accuracy) win, bounded by C2 false-passes. ON THE NATURE OF THE GAIN (corrected by the foreign-task-thinking ladder): the model USES thinking as content -- splicing a different task's thinking collapses accuracy to ~4% because the model follows it to the wrong problem; relevant-but-scrambled thinking ~= no thinking; coherent thinking adds +12pp. So at the efficient budget the behavioral gain IS coherent reasoning over relevant content. The earlier 'mostly compute/scaffold, not reasoning' read was overstated -- a greedy-metric artifact (greedy shuffle recovered ~1/3, sampled ~0) that holds mainly at HIGH budgets (2048 shuffle~=real, overthinking) and at the REPRESENTATIONAL level (separability probe: thinking raises decodability but shuffle>=real, and content-vs-compute decodability differences are small/noisy). The decomposition is now COMPLETE (filler/pause-token arm added): at the efficient budget pure compute (contentless '.' filler) = no_think (0.744 vs 0.749), scrambled relevant tokens = no_think (shuffle 0.739), and coherent content is the entire +0.122 (real 0.861); misleading content (foreign) -0.71. So pure compute buys ~0 and the efficient-budget gain is 100% coherent reasoning content -- the 'mostly compute/scaffold' reading was wrong for the efficient budget and survives only at high budgets / representationally.
claims.implication: Re-baseline CoT-substitute results against a fair, budgeted native-thinking baseline. Credit thinking as 'reasoning' only with content controls (foreign + filler); note the effect is budget-dependent (washes out when overthinking) and behavioral-vs-representational dissociated.
next_tests: High-budget (1024/2048) ladder to confirm the coherence advantage shrinks under overthinking.
Contamination-controlled / harder substrate where the no-think baseline is weaker (more headroom).
A learned controller using internal/uncertainty signals vs the visible-test C2 wall.
avoid: Claiming thinking gains are 'reasoning' without a content control that removes token-presence.
Pinning an exact optimal budget from single-seed n=100 gaps.
